name: Texas Dietitian License
description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Texas - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Dietitians Program
provider.parentOrganization: Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation (TDLR)

address.description:
Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation
P.O. Box 12157
Austin, TX 78711

requiredDocuments.name: Copy of current CDR registration card

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Texas dietitian license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The initial license application fee is $108, paid to TDLR. You must also pay a $34 fee to a third-party vendor to take the mandatory Texas Jurisprudence Examination, bringing the total to $142.

mainEntity.name: What is the Texas Jurisprudence Exam for Dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: It is a mandatory, online examination covering state laws, rules, and regulations relating to the practice of dietetics in Texas. All initial applicants must pass it.

mainEntity.name: Does Texas have reciprocity for dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Texas. To work in Texas, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Texas?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Texas while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.
